titleStmt.title: Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Edythe Fitzgerald, 3 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) to Edythe Fitzgerald expressing their deep sense of loss following the death of their colleague, Edythe's husband, Dr Charles Edward Fitzgerald (1853-1916).

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, 6 Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE ROYAL COLLEGE OF PHYSICIANS OF IRELAND,
6 Kildare street, Dublin. 3rd JUNE, 1916. Mrs.FitzGerald,
Dublin. Dear Madam, I am directed by the President and Fellows of this
College to forward you a copy of the following resolution
which was carried in silence at yesterday's meeting :
RESOLVED : "That the President and Fellows of the Royal
College of Physicians of Ireland desire to record their
deep sense of the loss sustained by their College, by the
Medical Profession and by the public at large in the death
of their distinguished and beloved colleague Dr.Charles
Edward FitzGerald, Ex-President of the College; and the
President and Fellows hereby tender an expression of
heartfelt sympathy to Mrs FitzGerald and the other members
of his family in their bereavement." I remain, Dear Madam,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar.
